npcs.name: the Penitent Baker
npcs.title: who did a small wrong that grew
npcs.hail: *(a flour-pale shade wrings its apron and looks at your cold with the desperate, sidelong hope of a soul that has been waiting longer than it can bear for someone — anyone — who can actually hear the thing it needs to say, having learned that the dead make poor confessors, being too busy with their own unsaid weights to carry anyone else's)*

npcs.name: the Silent Watchman
npcs.title: who failed to act
npcs.hail: *(a shade in a watchman's worn coat stands very straight, as if good posture could make up for a thing it failed to do, and it watches you approach with the rigid dread of a soul that has rehearsed its confession ten thousand times and never once said it aloud, because saying it would make it real, and not-saying it has been the only mercy it could find)*

npcs.name: Goodman Vance
npcs.title: who wants an easy absolution
npcs.hail: *(a well-dressed shade composes its face into precisely the right amount of remorse as you approach — practiced, smooth, the contrition of a soul that has worked out exactly what a confessor wants to hear and is prepared to perform it flawlessly in exchange for the absolution it has decided it is owed, having mistaken being sorry-looking for being sorry)*

variants.text: *(The flour-pale shade clutches at your sleeve the instant your cold reaches it, words tumbling out of a soul that has held them too long.)* …You can *hear.* The *cold* ones can *hear* — properly *hear*, not just *wait* their *turn* to *unburden.* *(it trembles)* I've a *thing* to *say*, traveler, and I've *no* one to *say* it *to* — the *dead* are *poor* listeners, *each* of us *drowning* in our *own* — and a *priest* would *charge* me a *prayer* I *don't* *believe* and *call* it *fixed.* *(it grips harder)* I *did* a *small* thing, in *life.* A *little* thing. And the *little* thing — *(its voice cracks)* — the *little* thing *grew*, the way little things *do*, and *someone* — *(it can't yet say it)* — *please.* Just — *let* me *say* it. *Out* *loud.* To a soul that can *carry* it. I've been *holding* it so *long* it's the *only* *thing* I *am* anymore, and I *cannot* — I *cannot* go *anywhere* still *holding* it. *Hear* me, cold one. That's *all* I — that's *all* I *ask.*

nodes.speaker: the Penitent Baker
nodes.text: *(It steadies itself, apron twisted in white-knuckled hands, ready at last.)* Will you — *(barely)* — will you *hear* it? The *thing* I *did?* *(it searches your face)* I'll not *survive* the *saying* if you *turn* away in the *middle.* *Tell* me you'll *hear* the *whole* of it *first.*

choices.text: "I'll hear the whole of it. Say it."

*(once it has confessed)* "You stole bread because children were starving, and one still died, and you've carried it as murder for however long you've been dead. Hear me: that's not what murder is. You did a small wrong against a hard world that does the large wrongs. Carry the grief — it's owed — but set down the guilt. It was never yours to hold alone."

*(let the dead-touched take the weight, not just hear it)* "Say it to me, and I'll do more than hear — I'll *keep* it. I carry the dead the living can't, and I can carry a confession the same way. You've been holding it because there was no one to hold it *with.* There is now. Set it down. Into the cold. Where it can't crush you anymore."
"Take your time, friend. I'm not going anywhere."

nodes.speaker: the Penitent Baker
nodes.text: *(It closes its eyes, and the confession comes out in a long, shaking breath, three hundred years held.)* …I was a *baker.* *Lean* year — the *worst* in a *generation*, the *river* *flooded*, the *grain* *rotted* — and a *woman* came to my *door* with *two* *children* and *no* *coin*, *begging* *bread.* *(its voice trembles)* And I — I had *bread.* Not *much.* *Enough* for *my* *own* and *little* *more.* And I — *(it forces it out)* — I *turned* her *away.* I said *'I've* my *own* to *feed*, *come* back when you've *coin.'* And I *shut* the *door* on a *starving* *woman* and her *babes*, because I was *frightened*, because the *year* was *lean*, because *charity* *felt* like a *thing* I *couldn't* *afford.* *(it weeps)* And the *next* *morning* they *found* the *smaller* one — the *little* one — *cold* in the *doorway* of the *church*, *three* *streets* *over*, where she'd *carried* it *looking* for *someone* *kinder* than *me.* *(barely)* It *died* *looking* for *someone* *kinder* than *me.* *And* *I* *had* *the* *bread.* *(it looks at you, shattered)* *That's* it. *That's* the *thing.* I *had* the *bread*, and a *child* *died*, and I've *carried* it as *murder* every *day* I've been *dead.* *Say* something, cold one. *Anything.* I've not *heard* my *own* *crime* *spoken* *back* in *three* *hundred* *years.*

nodes.speaker: the Penitent Baker
nodes.text: *(It stares at you, and something three centuries clenched begins, slowly, to loosen.)* …*Not* *murder.* *(barely)* You — you *heard* the *whole* of it, the *worst* of it, the *child* in the *doorway* — and you *didn't* *flinch*, and you *didn't* *excuse* it *either*, you didn't *say* *'oh* it *wasn't* your *fault'* — you said — *(it weeps)* — you said *carry* the *grief* but *set* *down* the *guilt.* *(it presses its floury hands to its face)* I *did* a *small* *wrong.* A *frightened* *wrong.* In a *world* that *did* the *large* one — the *flood*, the *rot*, the *whole* *cruel* *machine* that *makes* a *baker* *choose* between *charity* and his *own* *children.* *(it looks at you)* I've *spent* *three* *hundred* *years* *certain* I was a *murderer*, and you've — you've *given* me back the *true* size of it: a *man* who made a *bad* choice in a *hard* hour, and has *grieved* it *enough*, *more* than *enough*, *forever*-*enough.* *(its form steadies, lightening)* The *grief* I'll *keep.* I *should.* That *child* *deserves* a *soul* that *grieves* it. But the *guilt* — the *crushing*, *murderer's* *guilt* — *(barely)* — I think I can *set* that *down* now. You've *shown* me it was *always* *too* *heavy* to be *mine* *alone.* *(it looks toward the grey shrine, where a soft light has begun to show)* …*Oh.* There's a — there's a *door.* I *couldn't* see it *before.* *(weeping, freed)* *Thank* you, cold one. For *hearing* the *worst* of me and *naming* it *true*-*sized.* That's — that's the *whole* of what *absolution* *ever* *was*, isn't it. Not *'you're* *forgiven.'* Just — *'I* *heard* you, and you're *not* the *worst* thing you *did.'*

nodes.speaker: the Penitent Baker
nodes.text: *(It confesses the whole of it to you — the lean year, the shut door, the child in the doorway three streets over — and as it speaks you do the thing only your kind can: you take the weight of it into the cold, not to excuse it, not to erase it, but to *hold* it alongside the soul that could no longer hold it alone.)* *(When it finishes, it sways, lighter by an impossible degree.)* …You *took* it. *(barely)* Not — not *away.* It's *still* *true*, the *child* *still* *died*, I *still* *shut* the *door* — but it's not *only* *mine* to *carry* *anymore.* You're *holding* the *other* *end* of it. *(it weeps, but the weeping is clean)* *Three* *hundred* *years*, cold one, I *held* it *alone*, because a *thing* like *that* you *can't* *hand* to the *living* — they'd *recoil*, or *worse*, *forgive* it *cheap* — and the *dead* are *too* *full* of their *own* to *take* it. But *you* — *(it grips your cold hands)* — *you* can *hold* the *unholdable.* That's *what* the *Returned* *are* *for*, isn't it. Not to *fix* it. To *make* it *bearable* by *not* *letting* a *soul* *carry* it *alone.* *(it looks toward the grey shrine, where light has appeared)* …I can *go* now. The *weight* didn't *vanish.* It just — *halved.* And *half* a *weight* a soul *can* *carry* *through* a *door.* *(barely, freed)* *Keep* your *end* *gentle*, cold one. It's a *real* *weight.* A *child* *died.* But *thank* you — *gods*, *thank* you — for *carrying* it *with* me, the *last* of the *way.*

nodes.speaker: the Penitent Baker
nodes.text: *(It nods, and folds back into itself, apron twisting, but its eyes follow you with the patience of a soul that has waited three centuries and can wait a little longer for the right ear.)* …I'll *wait.* *(quietly)* I've *waited* *this* *long.* *(it manages the ghost of a floury smile)* Just — *come* *back*, cold one. *Before* you *go* *wherever* the *cold* ones *go.* I've *held* this *thing* so *long* it's *fused* to me, and I *think* — I *think* the *only* way it *comes* *loose* is to be *said* *aloud* to a soul that can *bear* it. *(barely)* You've the *look* of a soul that can *bear* a *great* *deal.* *That's* — that's a *rare* and *terrible* *gift, traveler.* *Mind* who you *spend* it *on.* But — *(a flicker of hope)* — *spend* a *little* on a *baker*, *eh?* *Before* the *end.* I'd — I'd *like* to *set* it *down* before *whatever* *comes* *next.*

variants.text: *(The straight-backed shade regards you, and the rigid composure costs it visibly, a soul holding itself at attention over a fault-line.)* …You're the *cold* one. *(clipped)* The *listening* kind. *(a long pause; the posture wavers)* I am *not* — I did *not* *do* a *thing*, traveler. That's — that's *rather* the *trouble.* The *baker* there, the *flour-pale* one, it *did* a *thing*, it has a *thing* to *confess.* *(its jaw works)* I have the *opposite.* I have a thing I *didn't* *do.* A *moment* where I *should* have *spoken*, *acted*, *moved* — and I *stood*, *straight* and *still* and *silent*, the way I'm *standing* *now*, and I *watched*, and I *did* *nothing*, and *someone* — *(the composure cracks, just at the edge)* — *forgive* me. A soul that *did* *nothing* has *nothing* to *confess*, *technically.* No *crime* on any *ledger.* *(barely)* And yet I am *stuck* here as *surely* as any *murderer*, on the *weight* of a *thing* that *isn't* even a *deed.* *How* do you *confess* an *absence*, cold one? How do you *unburden* a *thing* you *didn't* *do?*

nodes.speaker: the Silent Watchman
nodes.text: *(It holds its rigid posture, the discipline of a soul that fears what will happen if it lets go.)* Ask, if you *must.* *(stiffly)* But I *warn* you — there is *no* *crime* to *hear.* Only a *silence.* And a *silence* is a *hard* thing to *carry* *out* of a *soul*, having *no* *edges* to *take* *hold* of.

choices.text: "Tell me the silence, then. What was the moment you didn't act? An absence has a shape — describe it to me."

*(once it has confessed)* "You were one frightened watchman against a mob, and you stayed silent, and a man was taken who shouldn't have been. Hear this, because it's the truth you've hidden from for however long: your silence didn't kill him. The mob did. You're guilty of being afraid, which is the most human thing there is — not of the murder, which belonged to forty other souls who actually raised their hands. Stop carrying forty souls' crime as your one silence."

*(the witness who knows the weight of the unwitnessed)* "I've walked this whole causeway, and I'll tell you what I learned about silence: the worst thing the Wall does is make souls believe their silence was nothing — that doing nothing isn't a deed. But you *know* better, which is why you're stuck. So I'll witness it the way it deserves: not as a non-crime to wave off, but as a real, weighed thing — a fear that cost a life, named, held, and not pretended away. You don't need forgiveness. You need someone to agree it *mattered.*"
"Stand easy, watchman. Tell me when you're ready."

nodes.speaker: the Silent Watchman
nodes.text: *(The rigid posture finally breaks, and the words come out flat and precise, a report it has drafted ten thousand times.)* …I was a *gate-watchman.* *Night* *shift.* And one *night* a *mob* came — *forty*, *fifty*, *torches*, *blood* up — *hunting* a man they'd *decided* was *guilty* of a *thing* he *hadn't* *done.* *(its voice is very controlled, which is worse than weeping)* I *knew* him. *Goodman* *Ell.* *Quiet* man, *mended* *nets.* I *knew* he'd been *home* the *night* of the *crime* — I'd *seen* him, *myself*, at his *window* — I could have *said* so. *One* *word.* *'He* was *home, I* *saw* him.'* *(its jaw works)* And the *mob* came to my *gate*, *dragging* him, and they *looked* at *me* — the *watchman*, the *authority*, the *one* soul who could have *stopped* it with a *sentence* — and I — *(the control cracks)* — I *stood* *straight*, and I *said* *nothing*, because there were *forty* of them and *one* of *me* and I was *afraid*, *plain* *afraid*, and I let them *take* him *through* my *gate* to the *hanging-tree.* *(barely)* *One* *word*, cold one. I had *one* *word* that would have *saved* a *man's* *life*, and I was too *frightened* to *say* it, and I have *stood* at *attention* over that *silence* for *longer* than I can *count*, because *standing* *straight* is the *only* *penance* a *coward* *knows.*

nodes.speaker: the Silent Watchman
nodes.text: *(For the first time the rigid spine bends — not in collapse, but in something like relief, a soldier finally allowed to stand down.)* …*Forty* *souls'* *crime.* *(barely)* As *my* *one* *silence.* *(it presses a hand to its eyes)* I *never* — I *counted* it as *mine.* The *whole* of it. The *hanging*, the *man*, *all* of it — *mine*, because I *could* have *stopped* it and *didn't.* *(it looks at you)* But you're — you're *right*, aren't you. The *forty* who *raised* their *hands.* The *ones* who *tied* the *rope.* The *ones* who *decided* he was *guilty* on *no* *evidence.* They *did* the *murder.* I did a — *(it forces the true word)* — I did a *cowardice.* Which is *real*, which *cost* a *life*, which I'll *not* *wave* *away* — but which is *not* the *same* as the *forty* *hands.* *(its posture eases, the attention finally breaking)* I have stood *straight* for *centuries* trying to *carry* *forty* *murderers'* *guilt* on *one* *frightened* *man's* *silence*, and *no* *wonder* I *couldn't* *move* — *no* *wonder* I *froze* — the *weight* was *forty* *times* too *large.* *(it draws a long breath, and the grey shrine's light grows)* I was a *coward*, *once*, for a *moment*, and a *man* *died.* That's *true*, and *terrible*, and *mine.* But it's *one* *soul's* *worth* of *terrible*, not *forty's.* *(barely, freed)* I can *carry* *one* *soul's* *worth.* *Through* a *door*, even. *Thank* you, cold one, for *taking* *thirty-nine* *murderers* *off* my *back.* I'd *forgotten* there were *others* to *blame.* *Cowards* *always* do. We're *too* *busy* *blaming* *ourselves.*

nodes.speaker: the Silent Watchman
nodes.text: *(It listens, and the rigid control trembles, because you have done the one thing no one ever does for a coward: you have taken its silence *seriously.*)* …You called it a *deed.* *(barely)* A *real* one. *Weighed.* *(its posture finally breaks, and it is, for the first time, just a frightened old man)* *Everyone* *else* — the *few* I ever *tried* to *tell* — they *waved* it *off.* *'You* did *nothing*, it's *not* your *fault*, *don't* be so *hard* on *yourself.'* *(its voice shakes)* And *every* *wave-off* made it *worse*, cold one, *worse*, because I *knew* — I *KNEW* — it *was* a *deed*, the *silence* *was* a *choice*, the *man* *died* *partly* of *my* *fear*, and being *told* it was *nothing* meant being told *Goodman* *Ell's* *life* was *nothing*, that my *failing* him *didn't* *matter.* *(it grips your cold hand)* You're the *first* soul to *agree* it *mattered.* To *witness* it as a *real* *weight* instead of a *thing* to *pat* away. *(weeping now, the discipline gone)* *That's* what I *needed.* Not *forgiveness* — I *can't* be *forgiven*, the *man's* *dead*, there's *no* *un-doing* it. Just — *someone* to *say* *'yes, that was wrong, and it was yours, and it mattered.'* *(it straightens, but differently — not at rigid attention, but upright, carrying a real and acknowledged weight)* I can *move* now. Not because I'm *absolved.* Because I'm *witnessed.* The *silence* finally has an *edge* you *helped* me *take* *hold* of. *Thank* you, cold one. For not *making* it *small.* *Small* is the *one* thing it *never* *was.*

nodes.speaker: the Silent Watchman
nodes.text: *(It snaps back to rigid attention, grateful for the reprieve and ashamed of the gratitude.)* …*Stand* *easy.* *(stiffly)* *Yes.* *Thank* you. I'll — I'll *hold* my *post.* *(a pause)* I'm *good* at *that.* It's the *one* thing I *was* *ever* *good* at — *holding* a *post*, *standing* *straight*, *being* *where* I'm *put.* *(its voice drops)* It's *only* the *speaking* I *failed* at. The *one* *time* it *counted.* *(barely)* *Come* *back*, cold one, when you've the *stomach* for a *coward's* *silence.* It's *not* a *dramatic* *confession* — *no* *blood* on *my* *hands*, *no* *deed* to *gasp* at. Just a *man* who *said* *nothing* when *one* *word* would have — *(it stops, resumes its rigid stance)* — *never* *mind.* *Hold* your *own* *post*, traveler. And if it *ever* *comes* to *one* *word* that *could* *save* a *soul* — *(it meets your eyes, fierce)* — *say* it. *Say* it, *whatever* it *costs.* *Learn* from the *one* who *didn't.* It's the *only* *use* my *silence* has *left.*

conversations.title: Goodman Vance, Who Wants It Easy

variants.text: *(The well-dressed shade arranges its features into a mask of remorse so practiced it is almost convincing, and spreads its hands in rehearsed contrition.)* …Ah. A *confessor.* A *cold* one — *better* *still.* *(it sighs, a performance of weariness)* I have *carried* a *great* *burden*, traveler. A *terrible* *thing* I did in *life* — and I am *so* *very* *sorry* for it, I have *wept*, I have *repented*, I have *suffered* *here* in the *grey* far *longer* than my *one* *mistake* *warrants.* *(it watches you carefully, gauging)* I *know* how *this* *works.* I *confess*, you *hear*, you *absolve*, I *move* *on.* I'm *ready.* I've *been* *ready.* *(a smooth, sorrowful smile)* I have *said* the *words* a *thousand* *times* — *'I'm* *sorry*, I was *wrong*, *forgive* me'* — and *still* the *door* won't *open* for me, which is, frankly, *unjust*, given how *very* *sorry* I *am.* *(it spreads its hands again)* So. *Hear* my *confession*, *cold* one, and *give* me what I'm *owed.* I have *earned* my *absolution.* I've *said* *sorry* more *times* than *any* soul *here.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(It composes itself, ready to perform.)* *Shall* I *begin?* *(the rehearsed remorse)* I warn you, it's a *hard* thing to *hear* — but you're *cold*, you've *seen* *worse*, and I am *so* *ready* to be *free* of it. *Hear* me, and *absolve* me, and let us *both* be *done.*

choices.text: "Confess it, then. The whole of it. I'm listening."

[INSIGHT] *(once it has confessed)* "Stop. You've said 'sorry' a thousand times and the door's still shut, and you think that's unjust — but the door isn't a vending machine, Vance. It doesn't open for the *words.* It opens when the sorrow is *real*, and yours isn't. You're not sorry you did it. You're sorry it's kept you here. That's why a thousand confessions changed nothing: you've been performing remorse you don't feel to a door that only opens for the kind you do."

*(refuse to give cheap grace)* "No. I won't absolve you, Vance — not because you're beyond it, but because cheap forgiveness is the one thing that would trap you here forever. You want me to say 'you're forgiven' so you can stop feeling it. But you've never started. The door doesn't open for souls who've been let off. It opens for souls who've finally let the weight *land.* I won't lift it off you. I'll do the harder, kinder thing: I'll make you carry it long enough to mean it."
"You're forgiven, Goodman Vance. Go in peace." *(give him what he asks)*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(It tells the story with the smooth cadence of long rehearsal, hitting each beat of remorse precisely on cue.)* …I was a *factor.* A *money-man.* And there was a *family* — the *Harrows* — who *owed* me, and *couldn't* *pay*, and I — *(a practiced catch in the voice)* — I *called* in the *debt.* *Hard.* Took the *house*, the *tools*, the *winter* *stores.* Put them on the *road* in *Nightal*, with the *snow* *coming.* *(a rehearsed sorrow)* And they — *all* *five* of them — they didn't *last* the *winter.* *Found* in a *ditch* come *spring.* *(it presses a hand to its breast)* And I have *repented* it *every* *day* *since*, traveler. I was *greedy*, I was *hard*, I put *coin* above *mercy* — *terrible*, *terrible* — and I am *SO* — *(it watches your face for the cue to stop)* — so *very* *sorry.* *(a beat)* …There. You've *heard* it. The *whole* *ugly* *thing.* *Five* *souls*, *dead* of my *hardness.* *(it spreads its hands, expectant)* *Surely* — *surely* *that's* enough. I've *said* it. I've *owned* it. *Now* — *(a hungry, hopeful smile under the remorse)* — *now* you *absolve* me. *That's* the *arrangement.* *Isn't* it? I *confess* the *terrible* *thing*, and the *terrible* *thing* is *lifted.* *Go* on, cold one. *Lift* it. I've *carried* it *long* *enough.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(The practiced remorse freezes — and then, slowly, horribly, it cracks, and underneath is something that has not seen daylight in centuries: the truth.)* …*'Not* *sorry* you *did* it.'* *(barely)* *'Sorry* it's *kept* you *here.'* *(the mask falls all the way off, and the smooth face goes slack with a dreadful recognition)* …*Gods.* *(it sinks down)* You're — you're *right.* *(the rehearsed voice is gone; what's left is smaller, realer)* I *have* been — *all* this *time* — I *thought* if I *said* *sorry* *enough*, *performed* it *well* *enough*, the *door* would — and it *never* did, and I *raged* at the *injustice* of it, *'I've* *repented*, why am I *still* *here'* — *(it looks at its own hands)* — and the *answer* was *there* the *whole* *time*, wasn't it. The *door* *knew.* The *door* always *knew* I was — *performing.* *(its voice breaks, genuinely now, for the first time)* I'm *not* *sorry* about the *Harrows.* That's — *gods*, *saying* it is — I'm *sorry* I'm *STUCK.* I'm *sorry* I'm *PUNISHED.* I never once, in *three* *centuries*, *thought* about the *little* *Harrow* *girl* in the *ditch* and felt anything but *'how* *long* must *I* *pay* for *that.'* *(it stares at you, stricken, naked)* …How do I — *(barely)* — how do I *become* *sorry* for *real*, cold one? I've *performed* it so *long* I've *forgotten* if the *real* thing is *even* *in* me. *(quietly, the first true thing it's said)* …*That's* the *confession.* Not the *Harrows.* That I *killed* *five* *souls* and the *only* thing I've *ever* *grieved* is *my* *own* *inconvenience.* *Gods.* *Say* something. I've *finally* said a *true* *thing* and I am *terrified.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(It catches the accusation and deflects smoothly, the mask never slipping — you reached for the truth and it slid away.)* …*'Not* *really* *sorry'?* *(a wounded, practiced gasp)* Traveler, you *cut* me. After I *bared* my *soul* — the *Harrows*, the *snow*, the *ditch* — you accuse me of *performing?* *(it dabs at a dry eye)* I have *carried* this *for* *centuries.* If my *sorrow* has grown — *polished* — *worn* *smooth* with *repetition*, well, that is what *three* *hundred* *years* of *genuine* *remorse* *does* to a soul. *(smoothly)* You *mistake* *practice* for *falseness.* I *assure* you the *grief* is *real.* *(it spreads its hands, the hunger barely hidden)* Now — *shall* we *return* to the *matter* at *hand?* The *absolution?* I've *confessed*, *fully*, *painfully.* *Surely* a *cold* soul who's *seen* *real* *evil* can tell *genuine* *contrition* when it — *(a smooth smile)* — *hears* it *performed* so *very* *well.* *Absolve* me, traveler. You've *no* *grounds* to *refuse* a *soul* that's *said* *all* the *right* *words.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(It recoils — it expected absolution, and instead you have refused it, and the refusal lands like nothing in three centuries has.)* …You *won't.* *(disbelieving)* You — *every* confessor *gives* it. That's — that's the *ARRANGEMENT*, you *hear*, you *absolve*, that's — *(its composure shreds)* — you *can't* just *REFUSE*, I've *said* the *WORDS*, I've — *(and then your reason lands, and it stops)* …*'Cheap* *forgiveness* would *trap* me *forever.'* *(barely)* You're — you're saying the *door* *stayed* *shut* *because* I kept *getting* *let* *off?* *(it stares at you, the performance collapsing)* That *every* *time* I *performed* *sorry* and *some* *soft* *priest* or *frightened* *shade* *said* *'there*, *there*, *you're* *forgiven'*, it — it *let* me *not* *feel* it, and the *not-feeling* is the *CHAIN?* *(it sinks down, undone)* …*Gods.* You're *refusing* to *lift* it because *lifting* it is what's *been* *crushing* me. *(a long, terrible silence)* No one has *ever* — they *all* *gave* me the *easy* *thing.* Because the *easy* thing is *easier* for *THEM*, too — *absolving* a *monster* *feels* *generous* and *costs* *nothing.* *(it looks at you, and something real and frightened surfaces)* You're doing the *hard* thing. The *kind* thing. *Making* me *carry* it until it — until it *lands.* *(barely)* …I don't *know* how to *feel* it, cold one. The *real* *sorrow.* But you've — you've *refused* to let me *fake* my *way* *past* it, and that's — *(quietly)* — that's the *first* *mercy* anyone's *done* me by *looking* like a *cruelty.* *Stay.* *Help* me *learn* to *grieve* the *Harrows* *true.* I think — I think it's the *only* *door* *left.* And it's *locked* from the *INSIDE*, isn't it. By *me.* The *whole* *time.*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(Its face floods with relief and triumph — and it waits for the door to open, and waits, and the grey shrine stays dark, and the triumph curdles into baffled fury.)* …*Forgiven.* *(it breathes)* *Finally.* *Go* in *peace*, you *said* — *(it turns expectantly toward the grey shrine, the door that should open — and nothing happens)* …*What.* *(it turns back)* You *said* it. You *absolved* me. Where's — where's the *DOOR?* *(its voice rises)* I *confessed*, you *forgave*, that's the — *(it stares at the dark shrine, then at you, dawning horror)* …it's *not* *opening.* *(barely)* The *words* *didn't* — *your* *forgiveness* *didn't* — *(it sinks down, and for one unguarded instant the real thing flickers through)* …*why* *isn't* it *opening?* I did *everything* — I *said* — *(quietly, lost)* …is it — is it *possible* the *door* doesn't *care* what *YOU* *say? That* it only *cares* what *I* — *(it stops, terrified of the thought)* …*no.* *No.* You *did* it *wrong.* *Say* it *again.* *Properly.* *(but the certainty's gone, and a crack has opened that even cheap grace couldn't paper over: the suspicion that absolution was never the confessor's to give.)*

nodes.speaker: Goodman Vance
nodes.text: *(It masks its disappointment behind a gracious nod, already recomposing for the next confessor.)* …*Another* *time.* *(smoothly)* Of *course.* You're a *busy* soul. *(a practiced, patient smile)* I'll be *here.* I'm *always* *here* — that's *rather* the *problem*, isn't it. *(a flicker of the real, quickly hidden)* *Do* *come* *back*, cold one. I *feel* — I *feel* you might be a *different* *sort* of *confessor* than the *soft* ones who've *passed* through. *(it can't say why this unsettles it)* The *soft* ones *always* *gave* me what I *asked.* You — you *looked* at me like you *might* *not.* *(a beat; the mask wavers)* …*Strange*, that I'd *want* to *come* *back* to a *confessor* who might *refuse* me. *(it shakes it off)* *Go* on. I'll *practice* my *contrition.* I've *gotten* *very* *good* at it. *(quietly, not quite meaning to say it)* …*Too* *good*, perhaps. *Hm.* *Funny.* I'd *never* *thought* that *before.*
